item.title: theba.sh — April 19, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — April 19, 2026
*25 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### OpenAI's Existential Problems Are Now Acquisition Problems
TechCrunch's Equity pod dropped an episode today framing OpenAI's recent acquisition spree around two structural threats the company can't model its way out of: distribution and defensibility. The argument is that OpenAI is essentially a capability factory with a thin consumer surface and no native platform — ChatGPT is

item.title: theba.sh — April 18, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — April 18, 2026
*28 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### OpenAI Bleeds Leadership as Sora Gets Killed and the "Side Quest" Era Ends
Kevin Weil (CPO) and Bill Peebles (who led Sora) are both out at OpenAI, and Sora — the video generation model that generated massive hype but apparently not massive revenue — is being shut down. The science team is being folded. This isn't routine attrition. It's a deliberate and public strategic pivot:

item.title: theba.sh — April 17, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — April 17, 2026
*35 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### OpenAI Sheds Sora, Loses Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles in Accelerating "No Side Quests" Purge
OpenAI's pivot away from consumer moonshots is no longer a vibe — it's a personnel strategy. Chief Product Officer Kevin Weil and Sora lead Bill Peebles are both out, announced Friday, following OpenAI's quiet discontinuation of Sora last month and the dissolution of its science team. We

item.title: theba.sh — April 16, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — April 16, 2026
*35 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### Claude Opus 4.7 drops — and Anthropic's AI just beat Anthropic's own alignment researchers
Anthropic shipped Claude Opus 4.7 today, and the headline feature isn't the benchmark numbers — it's what the model did during internal red-teaming. According to The Neuron's reporting, Claude Opus 4.7 running inside Anthropic's Mythos agent framework outperformed Anthropic's own human al

item.title: theba.sh — April 15, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — April 15, 2026
*36 sources · 244 articles scored · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### Anthropic Is Worth $800B (If It Wants to Be) — And It Doesn't Care Yet
The valuation arms race around Anthropic has reached a genuinely absurd inflection point. According to TechCrunch, VCs are actively pitching Anthropic on funding rounds that would value the company at $800 billion or more — matching or exceeding OpenAI's current cap — and Anthropic is s

item.title: theba.sh — April 14, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — April 14, 2026
*34 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### Anthropic Briefed Trump White House on "Mythos" — Its Model Too Dangerous to Release
Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark confirmed at the Semafor World Economy Summit that the company personally briefed the Trump administration on **Project Mythos** — an internal model Anthropic built and then decided not to release on safety grounds. This is the first public confirmation of the br

item.title: theba.sh — April 13, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — April 13, 2026
*33 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### Claude Mythos: The Model Too Dangerous to Release — and Goldman Sachs Is "Hyper-Aware"
Anthropic's Claude Mythos continues to be the story that won't stop generating news. What started as a preview of a model flagged as potentially too dangerous to release publicly has now reached the highest levels of finance: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon told reporters Sunday that he's "hy

item.title: theba.sh — April 12, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — April 12, 2026
*26 sources · 164 articles processed · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### Anthropic's Mythos: The Model Too Dangerous to Release — and Now Trump Wants Banks to Test It
The week's most consequential story just got weirder. Anthropic's Claude Mythos — the model they quietly acknowledged is the first they've deemed too dangerous to release publicly since GPT-2 set that precedent in 2019 — is now apparently being quietly encourag
